preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial fricative [ɸ] to the voiceless glottal fricative [h] is a process of debuccalization (lenition). This transformation marks the loss of an oral place of articulation, moving the friction to the glottis.

examples.note: Historical trajectory: *p > ɸ > h. The modern Japanese /h/ is still realized as [ɸ] before [u].

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
